executive_brief: The Learning Platform API is a backend service built on a modern asynchronous Python stack. It implements a RESTful architecture under the /api/v1/ namespace, utilizing a standardized JSON envelope for all responses. The system enforces a strict role-based access control model (student and instructor) secured by short-lived JWT tokens and managed via asynchronous PostgreSQL persistence.

architectural_constraints: Real PostgreSQL instance for database tests (no mocking)
Mandatory explicit review for auth and response envelope changes

maturity_assessment: The project specifications are READY for execution. The architectural framework is highly stable, demonstrating comprehensive alignment between the core stack and the testing strategy. While a minor gap exists regarding the absence of a formal 'Open Questions' section, it does not impede the technical implementation of the defined constitution.
critical_dependencies: PostgreSQL 16 instance for runtime and testing
